info.title: Muapi API
info.description:
Generative-media API: text-to-image, image-to-video, video-edit, lipsync, audio, 3D, and LLMs — 500+ models behind a single key.

### Quickstart
```http
POST https://api.muapi.ai/api/v1/{model-endpoint}
x-api-key: YOUR_KEY
Content-Type: application/json

{ "prompt": "…" }
```
Returns `{ "request_id": "abc123" }`. Poll `GET /predictions/{request_id}/result` until `status: completed`, or pass `?webhook=https://you/cb` to be POSTed the result.

### Picking a model
Operations are grouped by **category** (text-to-image, video-edit, lipsync, …) and tagged with a **quality tier** in the summary:

- `best` — flagship pro/ultra/master variants (highest fidelity, slowest, most expensive)
- `balanced` — dev / standard mid-tier (good default)
- `fast` — turbo / klein / schnell (sub-second to seconds)
- `budget` — cheapest per-call (wan 2.x, hailuo standard, pixverse)

Each operation's description starts with `Action: … Input: … Output: … Use for: …` so an LLM can match user intent to model directly.

### Common conventions
- All generation is **async**: every POST returns a `request_id`; outputs land on `GET /predictions/{id}/result`.
- Status values: `queued`, `pending`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`.
- `webhook` query param turns any endpoint into push delivery.
- Costs are debited from the wallet on completion; `GET /account/balance` reports remaining credit.
- Sandbox keys (`is_test: true` on key creation) return mock outputs without billing.

### Auth
`x-api-key: <key>` on every request. Create keys via `POST /api/v1/keys` or `https://muapi.ai/account/api-keys`.

get.description:
Public catalog of every model available on muapi.ai with pricing.

Returns one entry per model with `name` (use as the endpoint slug —
`POST /api/v1/{name}`), human-readable `description` and `category`, the
base USD `cost` per call, and a `cost_strategy` flag.

Cost semantics:
  • `cost_strategy == "fixed_cost"` (or any strategy not in the dynamic
    registry) → `cost` is the exact USD price per call.
  • Otherwise → `cost` is a representative base; the final price depends
    on the request payload (duration, resolution, etc.). Call
    `POST /api/v1/models/{name}/estimate-cost` with the same payload you
    plan to submit to get the exact USD price.

Every generation response also includes the real charge as
`cost.amount_usd` plus the `X-MuAPI-Cost-USD` response header, so this
endpoint is for pre-flight pricing UI only.

get.description:
Pricing + metadata for a single model.

Returns the same shape as `GET /api/v1/models` for one model, plus the
full `input_schema` and `output_schema` so callers can introspect the
request payload without hitting `/openapi.json`.

Public changelog — most recently added models, sorted by created_at desc.

Automatically reflects every model synced via sync_schema_to_db.py.
No auth required.

post.description:
Quote the exact USD cost for a given model + request payload.

Body is the same JSON you would `POST` to `/api/v1/{name}`. For models
with dynamic pricing (video duration, resolution, audio length, etc.)
this runs the same cost function the billing system uses, so the quote
matches what you'll actually be charged.

For fixed-price models the base `cost` is returned.

Use this to surface a price preview to your end users before triggering
the generation.

get.description:
Public catalog of built-in muapi agent skill recipes.

Returns the name, description, inputs, triggers, and estimated cost for
every recipe shipped in `agent/skills/`. No auth — these are discovery
metadata so external agents (Claude, Cursor, custom LLM apps) can
enumerate which workflow recipes muapi.ai offers without needing an
account first. Use `GET /api/v1/agent-skills/{name}` to fetch the full
markdown body.

Full markdown body for a single built-in agent skill recipe.

Body is the natural-language recipe the agent reads + executes. Inputs
schema is also returned so clients can prompt the user for required
fields before invoking the recipe.

Generate talking head video from portrait image and audio using OmniHuman 1.5. Input: portrait image, audio. Output: talking head video. Use for: virtual presenter, avatar video, lipsync animation.

Generate a pack of diverse photos from single input. Input: image or prompt. Output: multiple photo variations. Use for: content batch generation, photo variety.

Combine/merge multiple videos into one. Input: multiple videos. Output: combined video. Use for: video stitching, compilation, montage creation.

Generate videos from text using Pixverse v6 (March 2026, 15s, 1080p, 20+ cinematic cameras, multi-shot, native audio). Input: text prompt. Output: 1080p 15-second video. Use for: cinematic video with camera controls, multi-shot videos.

Generate videos from text using Seedance 2 Fast (optimized for speed). Input: text prompt. Output: video, lower latency. Use for: rapid iteration, draft video generation.

Use for: cinematic generation with native sound, multilingual talking-head video, narrative shots where dialogue must lip-sync, premium video quality.

SunoVoiceCloneRequest.title: SunoVoiceCloneRequest
SunoVoiceCloneRequest.description:
Stage 1 of Suno custom voice cloning.

User uploads a short audio sample of their voice. Provider returns a fresh
random phrase the user must read aloud and submit via /suno-voice-clone/{id}/confirm.

SunoVoiceConfirmRequest.title: SunoVoiceConfirmRequest
SunoVoiceConfirmRequest.description:
Stage 2 of Suno custom voice cloning.

User submits a recording of themselves reading the phrase returned by
stage 1. On success, a reusable voice_id is generated.

tags.name: Image: Text-to-Image
tags.description: Generate images from text prompts. Pick based on speed/quality/cost — flagship (Imagen4, Seedream v5, Midjourney v8, Flux 2 Pro, Nano Banana Pro) for best quality; Schnell / Klein turbo / SDXL for fast & cheap.

tags.name: Image: Edit & Reference
tags.description: Edit, restyle, or reference-condition an existing image. Includes Flux Kontext (pro/max/dev), GPT-image edit, Seededit, Qwen edit, Nano Banana edit, Midjourney i2i.

tags.name: Video: Text-to-Video
tags.description: Generate video from a text prompt. Premium tier: Veo 3.1, Sora 2 Pro, Kling v3 Pro, Seedance Pro. Budget tier: Wan 2.x, Pixverse, Hailuo.

tags.name: Video: Image-to-Video
tags.description: Animate a still image. Same families as T2V but with image_url required. Use Kling Master / Seedance Pro for motion fidelity, Wan / Hailuo for cheap.

tags.name: Video: Edit & Effects
tags.description: Modify existing videos — face swap, dress change, watermarking, AI effects, storyboarding, video extension.

tags.name: Video: Lipsync
tags.description: Sync a video's mouth movements to an audio track. Sync, LatentSync, Creatify, Veed, Heygen translate.

tags.name: Video: Avatars
tags.description: Talking-head avatar generation (Kling Avatar v1/v2 standard & pro).

tags.name: Audio
tags.description: Music, voice, sound-effect, and audio-from-video generation. Suno family for music; MMAudio for SFX; Minimax Speech for TTS.

tags.name: Image: Enhance
tags.description: Upscale, background-remove, face-swap, skin-enhance, stylize (ghibli/anime/colorize/extend/erase/product-shot). One-call enhancements on an existing image.

tags.name: LLM / Multimodal
tags.description: Text and vision LLMs proxied through Muapi (Claude, GPT-5, Gemini 3, OpenRouter).

tags.name: 3D Generation
tags.description: Text-to-3D and image-to-3D model generation. Tripo3D and Meshy families output .glb meshes.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Create, list, and revoke API keys.

tags.name: Files
tags.description: Upload media to Muapi-hosted storage so other endpoints can reference it.

tags.name: Predictions
tags.description: Poll prediction status and retrieve outputs.

tags.name: Utilities
tags.description: Prompt helpers, autocrop, captions, moderation, YouTube download, transcription. Mostly text-in/text-out endpoints used as workflow pre-steps.
